t: modafinil, armodafinil, solriamfetol, pitolisant; stimulants (methylphenidate, amphetamine) in refractory cases.
sodium oxybate (Xyrem) — gold standard; new — calcium/magnesium/potassium/sodium oxybate (Xywav, FDA 2020, low sodium); SSRI/SNRI (venlafaxine, fluoxetine); pitolisant.
— GHB; two doses at night; effect on cataplexy and EDS.
— H3 receptor antagonist; for EDS and cataplexy.
Tetrad (EDS + cataplexy + paralysis + hallucinations); SOREMPs differentiating in MSLT.
consciousness preserved; EEG no epileptic activity; emotional trigger.
Myth 3: "H1N1 vaccine causes narcolepsy — vaccines should be avoided"
Risk indicated specifically with Pandemrix (2009 Scandinavia); other vaccines are safe; avoiding vaccination overall is not recommended.
Driving must be restricted in a narcoleptic patient with EDS.
